summary: Iteration 4 end-to-end testing for MIR Consulting Admin Password Reset / Forgot Password (magic-link) flow. 13/13 new pytest cases pass (test_admin_reset_password.py) covering login (200/401), forgot-password (admin-email success, non-admin email returns generic 200 with backend WARNING log, rate-limit 3/15min surfaces 429), reset token lifecycle (invalid 400, valid GET 200, POST consumes & rotates bcrypt hash, single-use enforced, old password stops working, new password works), and regression on protected admin endpoints (/leads /posts /case-studies /invoices /stats) + public POST /api/leads (still 201, new-lead email sent — confirmed in backend logs: 'Email sent: New consultation request'). Frontend: /admin login card shows admin-forgot-password-link; clicking opens admin-forgot-dialog with admin-forgot-email + admin-forgot-submit, submitting returns generic 'Check your inbox' success state, admin-forgot-close closes the dialog. /admin/reset/:token route works for both invalid (admin-reset-invalid state shown) and valid (admin-reset-form -> admin-reset-done) tokens. After programmatic valid-token reset to a temp password, login at /admin succeeds and all 4 admin tabs (Leads, Insights, Case Studies, Invoices) render with data. Admin password restored to mir-admin-2026 at end of run; test_credentials.md remains accurate. Backend SMTP confirmed live: live reset email queued ('Password reset link generated' + 'Email sent: Reset your MIR Consulting admin password').

minor.issue: Rate-limit is 5/min and shared across test suites. The legacy test_mir_invoices_i18n.py + test_mir_api_v2.py + test_mir_api.py had hardcoded ADMIN_PASSWORD='mir-admin-2025' which silently caused 5x 401 -> 6th request 429 cascading failures. Updated to 'mir-admin-2026' to match the current DB-stored password (task statement confirmed this is the current value).

issues: (carryover from iter3) Date inputs use browser-native <input type=date> instead of shadcn Calendar/Popover.
(carryover from iter3) Typo 'New case studie' should be 'New case study'.

action_items: (No-action / informational) /app/backend/.env still has ADMIN_PASSWORD="mir-admin-2025" as bootstrap-only seed. The live admin password lives in admin_settings (currently mir-admin-2026). Leaving the env mismatch is intentional per code comment, but consider updating ADMIN_PASSWORD to mir-admin-2026 just to remove the gotcha for future devs reading /app/backend/.env.

Three legacy pytest cases (test_email_status_unconfigured, test_invoice_send_unconfigured_smtp, test_create_lead_with_smtp_unconfigured in test_mir_invoices_i18n.py) now FAIL because they assumed SMTP_APP_PASSWORD is empty; the env now has a real Google App Password ('bugx fptj cngm rnao') so the API correctly returns 200/200/201 instead of 503/503/regression. These are stale test assumptions — main agent should either delete those 3 tests or invert their expectations to confirm SMTP IS configured.
(Cosmetic, carryover) Replace native date pickers in invoice editor with shadcn Calendar.
(Cosmetic, carryover) Fix 'New case studie' typo on Case Studies tab.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_admin.py — clean, focused (135 lines). Bcrypt hash starts with $2b$, SHA-256 hash on tokens (only hash stored, plain returned once), TTL index on expires_at + unique index on token_hash all wired in ensure_reset_indexes. Single-use is enforced via consume_reset_token's atomic update_one({used_at: None}) → second call returns modified_count=0 → 400.

server.py forgot-password endpoint: rate-limit '3/15minutes' is correct; non-admin email path returns generic {sent: True} AND logs WARNING — both verified end-to-end. SMTP send goes through BackgroundTasks so the HTTP response is fast (verified).

Token NOT exposed in any API response — confirmed by reading source and inspecting actual JSON bodies. Email link uses PUBLIC_BASE_URL/admin/reset/{plain_token}. PUBLIC_BASE_URL is set, so emails contain the correct URL.

/api/admin/reset-password/{token} GET returns expires_at — minor: this is fine because the token itself is required to query, so no enumeration risk, but consider returning only {valid: true} for the strictest hygiene.

Frontend AdminResetPassword.jsx has full state coverage (checking / invalid / form / done) with all required data-testids. Confirm-password is validated client-side; backend min_length=8 also enforced. Good.
Login endpoint rate-limit (5/min) is fine for product but is a footgun for test suites running back-to-back. Consider exempting localhost/internal IPs OR raise to 10/min in non-prod.

Legacy tests (test_mir_api.py / test_mir_api_v2.py / test_mir_invoices_i18n.py) hardcode passwords as module-level constants — fragile against password rotation. Recommend reading from /app/memory/test_credentials.md or an env var.

/app/backend/.env ADMIN_PASSWORD=mir-admin-2025 is out of sync with the actual live password (mir-admin-2026). Currently harmless because ensure_admin_seeded short-circuits on existing doc, but if admin_settings is ever wiped, the bootstrap would re-seed with the WRONG password.
server.py is now 928 lines — past 700 line threshold. Should be split into routers/ (admin_auth, leads, posts, case_studies, invoices).

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 reset-flow) — note 3 unrelated legacy SMTP-unconfigured tests now fail because SMTP got configured between iter3 and iter4; not regressions
success_rate.frontend: 100% (forgot dialog open/submit/close, invalid token state, valid token reset, post-reset login, all 4 admin tabs render)

test_credentials: Admin password: mir-admin-2026 (verified live, restored after rotation tests). Admin email: mirconsulting26@gmail.com. Static bearer: mir-admin-static-token-d7f3a91b5e6c4a8f.
seed_data_creation: Reset-token cycle created 1 password_reset_tokens row (marked used). 1 TEST_reset_*@example.com lead created via regression test (left in DB — acceptable, prefix tagged for cleanup). UI flow created 1 reset_token row (used). No invoice/post/case-study seed data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin password is mir-admin-2026 (live DB) — bootstrap env still says 2025. To test reset flow without inbox: cd /app/backend && python -c "import asyncio, sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); from motor.motor_asyncio import AsyncIOMotorClient; import auth_admin; asyncio.run((lambda: (lambda c, d: print(asyncio.run(auth_admin.create_reset_token(d))))(AsyncIOMotorClient('mongodb://localhost:27017'), AsyncIOMotorClient('mongodb://localhost:27017')['test_database']))())" — or just follow the pattern in test_admin_reset_password.py::TestResetTokenLifecycle. Rate limits to be aware of: /admin/login=5/min, /admin/forgot-password=3/15min, /admin/reset-password=5/15min — sleep 60-90s between back-to-back regression runs that re-authenticate. The 3 SMTP-unconfigured tests in test_mir_invoices_i18n.py are now stale — main agent decision needed on whether to delete or invert them.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — feature is fully working end-to-end. The only 'noise' (4 ERROR/FAILED in initial full-suite run) traced back to (a) legacy tests using mir-admin-2025 password — fixed by updating constants, and (b) SMTP_APP_PASSWORD now being set so SMTP-unconfigured assertions no longer hold — reported to main agent.
